name: Abayomi Community Development Corporation
description_short: Detroit-based nonprofit offering HUD-certified housing counseling, homebuyer education, youth development, and wellness services since 1998.
description_long:
Abayomi Community Development Corporation is a Detroit-based nonprofit organization founded in 1998 that serves as a HUD-certified housing agency and trusted community anchor. The organization was founded by Tawnya Morris and is currently led by President & CEO Hannah Morris, with a leadership team including a Director of Housing and Program Manager. Over two decades, Abayomi has built its reputation through direct partnership with Detroit residents facing housing insecurity and financial hardship.

Abayomi's core service offerings span four main pillars: Housing & Economic Development (homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, financial coaching, home repairs), Health & Wellness Initiatives (fitness classes, mental health counseling, trauma-informed care, youth empowerment), Business & Community Empowerment (small business support, workforce development), and Youth Development programs. Their flagship homebuyer education course is HUD-approved and runs every 4th Saturday of the month, with 48-hour advance enrollment required. The course equips first-time homebuyers and renters with certification often required for down payment assistance programs, one-on-one housing counseling, and tools to avoid predatory lending. They also operate a 36,000-square-foot facility (New St. Mark Family Life Center) offering event space rentals.

What distinguishes Abayomi is its culturally grounded, community-rooted approach emphasizing "love in action" and healing in community context. The organization explicitly serves Detroit residents with culturally prideful programming and long-term relational support rather than transactional services. Their leadership team is embedded in the community they serve, and client testimonials highlight the personal relationships and sustained support throughout the homebuying journey. The organization positions itself as walking alongside families through multiple life challenges simultaneously—housing, economic opportunity, youth development, and wellness.

Abayomi operates as a legitimate nonprofit with clear mission alignment and no apparent financial incentive conflicts. However, the website provides limited detail on program outcomes, success rates, or specific eligibility requirements beyond basic descriptions. As a free-help organization, their funding model and capacity limitations are not transparent. Prospective clients should contact them directly for detailed program availability, waitlist status, and specific eligibility criteria.
pros: HUD-certified housing agency providing government-approved homebuyer education certificates
Offers free homebuyer counseling and financial coaching alongside classroom education
Courses specifically designed to help clients access down payment assistance programs
Regular monthly course schedule (every 4th Saturday) with clear enrollment deadlines
Integrated approach addressing housing, health, youth, and economic development holistically
Leadership team embedded in Detroit community with 25+ year track record
Client testimonials demonstrate sustained support through entire homebuying process
cons: Website lacks specific data on program completion rates, client outcomes, or success metrics
Limited transparency on current waitlists, program capacity, or enrollment restrictions
No detailed eligibility criteria provided on website; requires direct contact for specifics
Event venue rental appears to be secondary mission focus rather than core programming
Website does not specify geographic service area beyond 'Detroit and surrounding communities'
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Detroit seeking HUD-approved education and down payment assistance qualification
Renters preparing for homeownership who need comprehensive financial literacy and mortgage readiness training
Families experiencing housing insecurity seeking foreclosure prevention counseling and financial coaching
Detroit residents seeking integrated wellness, youth development, and economic empowerment services
diagnosis: Abayomi is best for Detroit-area first-time homebuyers and renters who need HUD-certified homebuyer education to qualify for down payment assistance, plus families seeking integrated community support across housing, wellness, and youth development. The primary caveat is that program-specific details (waitlists, exact eligibility, current capacity) require direct contact, and the website provides limited outcome data to assess program effectiveness.
services: Mental health counseling and trauma-informed care
Civic engagement and community partnership programs
Event space rental at New St. Mark Family Life Center
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
